Web verification: Downloaded DICOM dataset from https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip (24.3 MB, 108 DICOM files in 0051/ directory). Code analysis: setup_task.sh creates symlink /home/ga/DICOM/ct_cranium → /opt/invesalius/sample_data/ct_cranium

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a real, publicly available CT Cranium DICOM dataset (108 slices) sourced from https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip. The task involves creating a 3D skull mesh from CT data with smoothing (≥15 iterations) and decimation to meet a 500,000 triangle limit for 3D printing. Output files are saved to /home/ga/Documents/ in PLY and binary STL formats, with the project saved as a gzip-compressed tar archive (.inv3). All output path specifications and triangle count thresholds are verified across task setup, export, and verification scripts.
